skill.name: sop-product-launch
skill.description: Product launch SOP that coordinates research, build, marketing, sales, launch, and post-launch monitoring workflows.
skill.summary: Coordinate a 10-week product launch with staged multi-agent workflows and deliverables.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review shows these are false positives from Markdown and JavaScript workflow examples. No malicious prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or hidden executable payload was found. The main residual risk is that the SOP describes production deployment and marketing operations that should require human approval.

low_findings.title: False Positive: JavaScript Template Literals Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backticks as Ruby or shell execution. Review confirms they are Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals inside documented Task examples, not executable shell commands in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines occur inside fenced JavaScript examples and Task prompt strings. No shell command invocation, Ruby execution, or standalone script file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Ordinary Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are substring matches in descriptive prose such as the skill description, database design text, and help desk setup text. They do not reference MD5, DES, SHA-1, or cryptographic implementation choices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found ordinary planning language, not cryptographic code or algorithm selection. The skill contains no implementation files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Matches Marketing Language
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings occur in product launch and paid advertising terminology. These lines describe campaign planning and success metrics, not host, network, or system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching terms appear in a business workflow context. There is no command, API call, or instruction to inspect a local or remote system.

low_findings.title: Operational Actions Require Human Approval
low_findings.description: The SOP includes launch execution steps for production deployment, app store submission, database configuration, and paid campaigns. These are legitimate launch tasks, but users should review and approve them before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The operational steps are explicit in the SOP and could affect production systems or budgets if followed by an autonomous agent. The content is not malicious, so this remains a low-severity governance concern.

content.value_statement: Product launches often fail because teams miss dependencies across research, engineering, marketing, sales, and support. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a staged SOP for coordinating launch work and deliverables.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a product launch into five phases across a 10-week timeline.
Defines sequential and parallel workflows for research, build, marketing, sales, launch, and monitoring.
Lists specialist agent roles and the deliverables each role should produce.
Provides memory namespace patterns for storing and retrieving launch artifacts.
Includes success metrics for technical, business, marketing, and support outcomes.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts, templates, or automation files beyond the SOP text.
Assumes access to Task-style agent orchestration and memory tools.
Uses placeholder targets, budgets, and product details that users must customize.
Does not replace legal, security, finance, or production approval processes.

use_cases.title: Plan a Startup Launch
use_cases.description: Create a structured launch plan that connects product strategy, development, marketing, sales, and support.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Cross-Functional Delivery
use_cases.description: Assign launch work across specialist agents and track expected deliverables for each phase.

use_cases.title: Prepare Launch Operations
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to organize deployment readiness, campaign execution, support setup, and post-launch reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Launch Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sop-product-launch skill to create a 10-week launch plan for [product]. Include phases, owners, dependencies, and key deliverables.

prompt_templates.title: Customize Agent Responsibilities
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the product launch SOP for our team size and tools. Map each workflow step to our available roles and identify missing owners.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Launch Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SOP to build a launch readiness review for [product]. Highlight blockers, approval gates, metrics, and rollback planning.

prompt_templates.title: Run Post-Launch Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the post-launch monitoring phase to our metrics. Prioritize optimization work across product, marketing, sales, and support for the next two weeks.

output_examples.input: Create a launch plan for a B2B analytics dashboard.
output_examples.output: A phased plan covering market research, MVP build, campaign preparation, launch execution, support readiness, and weekly optimization reviews.

output_examples.input: Prepare a go or no-go review for launch week.
output_examples.output: A readiness checklist with test status, security review, performance targets, monitoring, rollback plan, support coverage, and approval owners.

output_examples.input: Summarize post-launch metrics and next actions.
output_examples.output: A weekly report outline covering adoption, conversion, revenue, campaign results, support volume, risks, and prioritized improvement tasks.

best_practices: Customize timeline, success metrics, and deliverables before assigning work to agents.
Require human approval for deployment, paid advertising, pricing, and customer communications.
Store launch artifacts in clear namespaces so later phases can retrieve the right context.
anti_patterns: Do not execute the full SOP without product-specific requirements and business targets.
Do not let agents deploy to production or spend ad budget without explicit approval.
Do not treat placeholder metrics or generic role names as final launch commitments.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan and coordinate a product launch across research, product, engineering, marketing, sales, support, and monitoring.

faq.question: Does it run launch automation by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is a documented SOP with example orchestration prompts, not a package of executable automation scripts.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can I use it for a smaller launch?
faq.answer: Yes. Reduce the timeline, remove unneeded roles, and keep only the deliverables that match your launch scope.

faq.question: What should I review before execution?
faq.answer: Review deployment steps, paid campaign actions, pricing decisions, compliance checks, and all customer-facing communication.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It includes security audit and validation steps, but it does not replace a qualified security review.
